clinical_history: 63 year-old gentleman in Emergency Department with complaint of chest pain. What is the most likely diagnosis?
diagnosis: This ECG shows sinus rhythm, rate 90 bpm with profound subendocardial ischemia (marked ST segment depressions in leads V2-4 with T wave inversions in leads V3-6) consistent with acute anterior ischemia. Q waves in leads II, III and aVF are consistent with prior inferior myocardial infarction (MI).  This patient had a long history of severe vessel coronary artery disease including several angioplasties and previous MIs. Cardiac catheterization revealed a left ventricular ejection fraction of 35% and 3 vessel coronary artery disease. He then underwent successful coronary artery bypass graft surgery.
